items.title: Engineering Malaysia's new energy transition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In industrial facilities, steam energy systems often work quietly behind the scenes, ensuring operations run efficiently and reliably every day. Today, she leads the design of biomass steam energy systems that deliver continuous, high-pressure steam to industrial users while supporting Malaysia's renewable energy ambitions and advancing decarbonisation initiatives through biomass utilisation. As International Women's Day 2026 highlights the importance of greater representation in leadership across industries, See's role illustrates how women are increasingly shaping technical leadership in sectors critical to the energy transition.

items.title: Firms urged to adopt flexible work arrangements amid global economic pressures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Private sector employers are encouraged to adopt flexible work arrangements (FWA), including working from home, in line with amendments to the Employment Act 1955 that came into force on Jan 1, 2023. The Human Resources Ministry said the amendments allow employees to apply for flexible working arrangements, strengthening modern workplace practices that are more responsive to current needs. "These provisions allow employees to submit applications to employers for flexible arrangements in terms of working hours, working days, or place of work, including working from home," the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Four homes gutted in Batu Ferringhi fire, access and water issues hamper operations
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Firefighters battling a blaze that destroyed four double-storey semi-permanent houses in Kampung Din Manbo, Batu Ferringhi, today faced significant challenges due to narrow access routes and low water pressure. Despite the constraints, the Penang Fire and Rescue Department responded swiftly after receiving an emergency call at 11.29am. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said the fire involved four semi-permanent houses measuring about 40 by 50 square feet each.

items.title: VS Industry sinks to lowest since 2021 share split
items.summary: The stock fell as much as five sen or 17.54 per cent to 23.5 sen, its lowest level since the company's 2-for-1 share split in May 2021, before settling at 24 sen at midday, down 4.5 sen or 15.79 per cent. At 24 sen, VS Industry's market capitalisation stood at about RM947.45 million. Revenue fell 15.32 per cent to RM769.53 million from RM908.8 million previously.

items.title: George Kent issues RM80mil sukuk to strengthen capital structure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: George Kent (Malaysia) Bhd has issued RM80 million in nominal value sukuk under its sukuk wakalah programme, as part of its ongoing efforts to optimise its capital structure. The company said the sukuk reflects its continued access to the capital markets. "George Kent remains focused on prudent capital management and will continue to evaluate funding options that align with its strategic objectives and enhance long-term shareholder value," it added.

items.title: Lorry with modified tank leads police to 2,000-litre diesel stash in Kedah, suspect arrested
items.summary: JITRA, March 27 — Police have arrested a man and seized 2,250 litres of subsidised diesel believed to have been misappropriated in an operation, codenamed Op Tiris, at a petrol station in Bukit Kayu Hitam last Wednesday. Kubang Pasu police chief Mohd Radzi Abdul Rahim said the 32-year-old local man was detained at about 10am along with a lorry found in suspicious circumstances. “Inspection of the vehicle uncovered several items linked to diesel misappropriation activities, including subsidy cards belonging to other companies and a modified additional tank containing 250 litres of fuel,” he said at a press conference.

items.title: HLIB sees stronger sales momentum for MCE on new model launches
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) anticipates a significant boost in MCE Holdings Bhd's sales in the second half, supported by several key factors. "While the value per car remains broadly unchanged, stronger demand for the new model should translate into higher revenue contribution. "Secondly, MCE will also see a full-quarter contribution from Perodua Traz from Q3 FY26 onwards.

items.title: Police: Bystander slashed in KLCC melee after stepping in to stop alleged assault on tourist, 18 ‘photo touts’ held
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Police have arrested 18 men following a violent altercation near Kuala Lumpur City Centre (KLCC) that began with an alleged assault on a foreign tourist and escalated into a chaotic group attack, Dang Wangi police chief Sazalee Adam said in a statement today. Police said the victim sustained injuries to the head. Sazalee said all 18 suspects, aged between 17 and 45, were detained in an operation carried out in the vicinity of KLCC the following day.

items.title: 850kg of subsidised cooking oil seized in Kelantan raid, three arrested
items.summary: PASIR MAS: Enforcement officers have seized 850kg of subsidised cooking oil worth RM2,125 in a raid on a house in Kampung Lubuk Tapah, Kelantan. The suspects were detained while transferring boxes of cooking oil into the house, which is believed to have been used as a transit point. Officers seized the cooking oil and the van.

items.title: Impersonation scams leave two Penang company directors nearly RM900,000 poorer, say police
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — Two company directors in Penang have lost a combined total of nearly RM900,000 after falling victim to separate phone scam syndicates involving impersonation tactics. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the first case involved a 69-year-old woman who lost RM457,607 after being deceived by individuals posing as officers from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and the police. The victim, who was at her home in Simpang Ampat, received a call on March 1 from a man claiming her bank account was linked to a criminal case.

items.title: Receding Pedu Dam reveals submerged 50-year-old ancestral graves
items.summary: The area, formerly known as Kampung Mong Gajah, was among the settlements inundated during the dam's construction around 1974. Whenever the water recedes to expose the old burial grounds, descendants from Kampung Pinang, Kampung Tong Pelu, and Kampung Pedu gather to hold tahlil prayers for their relatives. [[nid:1404645]] Angler Zakaria Abdul Hamid, 63, said the receding water had exposed the graves, similar to a dry spell two years ago.

items.title: Trump bars firms with pro-diversity policies from govt contracts
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump issued an executive order Thursday prohibiting companies doing business with the federal government from having policies meant to combat racism or sexism in the workforce. The order, to come into effect in 30 days, is Trump's latest move against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, a series of often decades-old measures meant to combat racism and sexism across the United States. "My Administration has made significant progress in ending racial discrimination in American society, including so-called "diversity, equity, and inclusion" (DEI) activities," Trump wrote in the order.

items.title: Ten years’ jail, five strokes for man who raped vulnerable PwD woman in Skudai
items.summary: Judge Madihah Zainol handed down the sentence after the accused, R. Sivanesan, pleaded guilty when the charge was read out by a court interpreter. In mitigation, Omar Kutty told the court that the accused had apologised and was remorseful for his actions. “The accused took advantage of the victim who has a physical disability, namely speech, hearing and learning disabilities.

items.title: Ministry: No wrongdoing in fishermen fuel purchase
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has cleared a petrol station in Penaga of any wrongdoing following a viral video alleging suspicious fuel purchases. Penang ministry director S. Jegan said enforcement officers conducted an inspection and confirmed the Shell station is licensed by the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM) to supply subsidised diesel to fishermen under the e-Nelayan card system. An 18-second video has gone viral showing several man filling petrol into plastic cans placed in their boot.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Govt keeps RON95 at RM1.99 to shield Malaysians from rising costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Madani government remains committed to protecting the people from global economic pressures by maintaining the subsidised price of RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre under the BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) programme, keeping Malaysia among countries with the lowest fuel prices globally. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the move comes as global crude oil prices have surged beyond US$100 per barrel, affecting many countries. “However, in Malaysia, the Madani government under the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken a clear stance — the people must be protected.

items.title: 2023 heat guidelines sufficient for schools, says Education Ministry
items.summary: GOPENG: The existing guidelines on managing schools during hot weather are sufficient to safeguard students, Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said. He said the guidelines, introduced in 2023, already provide clear direction to ensure students' safety and comfort during periods of high temperatures, with monitoring by schools and district education offices. "School leaders have the experience and judgment to advise students appropriately based on the situation," he said.

items.title: Redouble efforts for a resilient and diversified energy future
items.summary: There is nothing like an energy crisis to grip global attention as a spike in oil prices affects just about everybody, everywhere. Still, there is no room for any complacency even if Malaysia is a net energy exporter. Many Asian countries, heavily reliant on energy imports, have lately scrambled to fall back on coal as an energy source.

items.title: Newborn baby found dead near Kuala Pilah mosque, police investigate
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 27 — The body of a full-term baby boy, still with the umbilical cord attached, was found near a telecommunications tower substation close to Masjid Rembang Panas in Kuala Pilah yesterday. Kuala Pilah District Police deputy chief DSP Shamrol Shah Ramli said police received a call from a local man at 6.30pm, reporting the discovery, before a formal report was lodged about half an hour later. “Following that, police went to the scene, and an initial check found the baby had died about two days earlier and had begun to decompose.

items.title: Security guard with HIV, leukaemia jailed 10 years for raping disabled woman
items.summary: Judge Madihah Zainol handed down the sentence after the accused, R. Sivanesan, 39, pleaded guilty to the charge. Earlier, Omar Kutty told the court that the accused was remorseful and sought forgiveness for his actions. "The accused earns RM2,200 a month.

items.title: SUPP will resolve internal issues, says party leader amid speculation over Lily Yong’s sacking
items.summary: KUCHING, March 27 — The Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) will be able to resolve any internal issues accordingly, said Datuk Wee Hong Seng. The Kuching South mayor called for restraint and adherence to due process following speculation over SUPP Kuching chairman Datuk Lily Yong Lee Lee’s sacking. “I’m pretty sure that a party such as SUPP with a long history will be able to resolve internal matters themselves.

items.title: Sarawak fire department warns of sharp spike in open burning cases
items.summary: Its deputy director Tiong Ling Hii said the latest statistics showed a worrying upward trend, particularly between March 22 and 25. Tiong said most cases involved agricultural clearing and deliberate burning. Miri was identified as the most critical division with 59 open fire cases, nearly double the 29 cases recorded in Sibu.

items.title: Election Court upholds Ruddy Awah's victory in Pitas state seat
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Election Court has struck out a petition filed by Warisan candidate Datuk Harun Ismail, challenging the results of the Pitas state seat won by Gabungan Rakyat Sabah's (GRS) Datuk Seri Ruddy Awah. Kuching-based High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, sitting as the election court judge, made the decision via Zoom today after allowing preliminary objections raised by Ruddy and two other respondents. Harun received 4,189 votes.

items.title: Newborn's body found near telecommunications substation in Kuala Pilah
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: The body of a newborn baby boy, with his umbilical cord still attached, was found near a telecommunications substation near Masjid Rembang Panas here yesterday afternoon. Kuala Pilah deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Shamrol Shah Ramli said police received a report from a local man regarding the discovery at 6.30pm. "Police are investigating the discovery of the unidentified infant found near the substation.

items.title: Dry spell drives villagers to Golok River for daily needs
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Villagers along the Malaysia-Thai border are turning to Sungai Golok to bathe and carry out daily chores as water levels continue to fall following a prolonged dry spell. Families, including children, were seen gathering along the riverbanks as early as 9am on Friday, bringing pails, soap, and laundry to make use of the accessible water source. For many residents, the river has become a temporary alternative amid dwindling well water levels and reduced domestic supply.

items.title: MEF urges return to WFH, carpooling to combat Malaysia’s rising fuel costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) is urging businesses to implement work-from-home (WFH) arrangements for non-critical roles and encourage carpooling as immediate, practical steps to support the government’s new fuel rationalisation measures. MEF president Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman said that since Malaysia cannot be insulated from these external pressures, managing fuel consumption must be a shared national responsibility. While backing the government’s policy, Syed Hussain highlighted the concerns of businesses, particularly the impact of rising operational costs on logistics and manufacturing.

items.title: Senior citizen loses RM1 million to 'Singaporean' love scammer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 69-year-old woman from Penang has reportedly lost more than RM1 million after falling victim to a love scam involving a man posing as a Singaporean engineer based in the United Kingdom. "The victim reportedly met the suspect online. He claimed to be a Singaporean working as an engineer for a consultancy firm in the UK," Kuan said.

items.title: RM13mil floating mosque in Yan set for completion next month
items.summary: YAN: The floating mosque in Kampung Ruat, scheduled for completion next month, is set to become one of the district's main attractions. Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said the project, taken over by a new contractor in July 2023, involved a total construction cost exceeding RM12.95 million. Ahmad said the main scope of work included the construction of the mosque building and supporting structures such as an arch, water tank tower, azan tower, a compact Tenaga Nasional Bhd substation, and a Small Sewage Treatment System.

items.title: Sarawak Bomba calls for immediate halt to open burning as fire cases spike in March
items.summary: SIBU, March 27 — The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has urged the public to stop the practice of open burning following a sharp increase in open fire cases in the state during mid to late March 2026. In addition, Bintulu (24 cases) and Mukah (23 cases) also showed a moderate risk level that required close monitoring. Kuching recorded 11 cases, followed by Betong (eight cases) and Sri Aman (five cases).

items.title: Election court strikes out Karambunai petition, PAS's Aliakbar retains seat
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Election Court has struck out a petition seeking to nullify the result of the Karambunai state seat in the 17th Sabah general election. PAS candidate Datuk Dr Aliakbar Gulasan remains the Karambunai assemblyman. Kuching-based High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, sitting as the election court judge, delivered the decision via Zoom after allowing all preliminary objections raised by the respondents, including Aliakbar.

items.title: Syndicate misappropriating RON95 petrol crippled near Gerik
items.summary: IPOH: The Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has crippled a syndicate involved in the misappropriation of subsidised RON95 petrol following the arrest of three men in Pengkalan Hulu, near Gerik, yesterday. The operation targeted vehicles suspected of being modified to store fuel. Authorities also seized three vehicles believed to have modified fuel tanks, along with 450 litres of RON95 petrol, with a total estimated value of RM15,741.50.

items.title: Multi-agency operation launched to contain 16-hectare bushfire near Sibu driving institute
items.summary: SIBU, March 27 — An inter-agency operation has been launched on Friday to contain a bushfire near the Ngu Chung Beng Driving Institute, which has burned about 16 hectares of scrubland. Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia (Bomba) Sibu Zone 4 Chief, Andy Alie said the response has been divided into three sectors, A, B and C, with a total of 50 personnel mobilised to support Bomba from the Sibu Central station. He said the operation strategy this morning required teams to cross a small stream before carrying out defensive and direct-attack methods to curb the fire’s spread.

items.title: Three Thai nationals nabbed smuggling petrol at Rantau Panjang ICQS
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers detained three Thai nationals for attempting to smuggle petrol into Thailand today. In the first case, a 53-year-old man was detained with 50 litres of petrol, with the total seizure valued at RM5,193.50. The third case involved a 35-year-old woman found with 50 litres of petrol and a car valued at RM5,193.50.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies after late-night crash involving fertiliser lorry in Mukah
items.summary: MUKAH, March 27 — A 30-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a road accident at KM20 of Jalan Mukah-Balingian late Thursday night. Mukah police chief Muhamad Rizal Alias said the fatal crash occurred at about 11pm on March 26 and involved a 25-tonne lorry carrying fertiliser and a motorcycle. The lorry driver escaped unhurt.

items.title: Too hot to work? Malaysia’s labour unions push for legal protection amid heat spell
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — A coalition of trade unions is demanding that the government legally recognize extreme heat as a deadly occupational hazard and enforce mandatory work stoppages during severe heatwaves, arguing that current voluntary guidelines leave millions of outdoor workers unprotected. “They are the most vulnerable group to prolonged heat exposure, yet their plight remains glaringly absent from mainstream public health discussions,” BWI-MLC said. This plan must include legally guaranteed compensation so workers are not penalised with wage cuts for safety-related work suspensions, the group said.

items.title: Floating mosque in Yan set to open in April, tipped as key tourism draw, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: YAN, March 27 — The floating mosque in Kampung Ruat here, the project scheduled for full completion next month, is set to become one of the district’s main attractions. Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said the project, taken over by a new contractor in July 2023, involves a total construction cost exceeding RM12.95 million. “In addition, the mosque has the potential to emerge as a new tourism attraction for the state,” he said after inspecting the progress of the floating mosque project here today.

items.title: Fadillah: Govt assessing nuclear energy potential to boost long-term security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is undertaking a comprehensive assessment of its potential nuclear energy programme, covering policy development, regulatory frameworks, project feasibility, industry participation, stakeholder engagement and human capital development. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the move to explore nuclear energy for electricity generation is a strategic effort to strengthen long-term energy security while supporting the country's clean energy transition. MyPower Corporation Malaysia, an agency under the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), has been appointed as the Nuclear Energy Programme Implementing Organisation (Nepio), tasked with coordinating preparations based on the phased approach recommended by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA).

items.title: Syndicate siphoning RON95 petrol crippled in Perak enforcement operation, three arrested
items.summary: IPOH, March 27 — The Perak enforcement division of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living has crippled a syndicate involved in the misappropriation of subsidised RON95 petrol following the arrest of three local men in an operation in Pengkalan Hulu near Gerik yesterday. Its director, Kamalludin Ismail, said the operation, codenamed Ops Tiris and carried out in collaboration with the Road Transport Department, targeted vehicles suspected of being modified for illegal fuel storage. Authorities also seized three vehicles believed to have modified fuel tanks, along with 450 litres of RON95 petrol, with a total estimated value of RM15,741.50.

items.title: Two Penang company directors lose RM890,000 to phone, investment scams
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Two company directors in Penang have lost a combined RM890,837.91 after falling victim to separate phone and investment scams. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the cases, reported on March 26, were being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating. "The victim received a call on March 1 while at her home in Simpang Ampat.

items.title: MEF calls for proactive fuel conservation, sustainable energy usage amid current uncertainties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: THE Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) emphasises that all segments of society must play a proactive and responsible role in conserving fuel and ensuring sustainable energy usage amid ongoing uncertainties in the Middle East. "MEF fully supports the government's fuel rationalisation efforts and calls on all stakeholders to act decisively and responsibly in managing fuel consumption," he said in a statement today. Looking ahead, MEF also encourages businesses to accelerate their transition towards sustainable practices and alternative energy solutions, he said.

items.title: Post-Hari Raya: When family homes fall quiet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As Aidilfitri ends, the warmth and bustle of family homes give way to quiet emptiness, leaving many to face the stillness of returning to daily routines. A TikTok video posted by @manchong captured the poignant moment, showing the calm of his house as family members departed. Last week, families celebrated Hari Raya, and this week many have begun returning home.

items.title: Sabah has right to diesel subsidies given key national O&G role, says deputy chief minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — Sabah is entitled to receive diesel subsidies given that the state is among the country’s main producers of oil and gas (O&G), said Datuk Ewon Benedick. “We appreciate the federal government’s decision to provide diesel subsidies to Sabah. “We defended the decision because Sabah is a state or region that produces gas and oil.

items.title: CIMB to develop Islamic financial instrument under SC innovation lab
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Holdings Bhd (CIMB) has announced plans to develop an innovative financial instrument aimed at strengthening Malaysia's Islamic capital markets, in line with Securities Commission Malaysia's (SC) Capital Market Masterplan 2026-2030. The initiative will be carried out in collaboration with SC and Bursa Malaysia Bhd under a pilot project within SC's ICM Innovation Lab (FIKRALab). CIMB group chief executive officer Novan Amirudin said the collaboration reflects CIMB's commitment to innovation and its support for SC's long-term vision for the capital market.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM MARCH 27, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Friday, outperforming regional peers as market sentiment improved following the government's adjusted fuel subsidy measures. The move supported fiscal stability and eased cost pressures on households amid ongoing West Asia uncertainties. In the crypto market, Bitcoin rose to RM275,750.

items.title: RHB sees 19pct earnings growth for Binastra, keeps "Buy" call
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Research has projected a three-year earnings compound annual growth rate of 19 per cent for Binastra Corp Bhd from financial year 2026 to financial year 2029 (FY26-FY29). The firm said it has kept its earnings forecasts unchanged as Binastra's results met expectations while introducing its FY29 earnings projections, which factor in a new job win target of RM2.5 billion. "Looking ahead, Binastra is targeting to secure over RM2 billion in new jobs in FY27, while our internal replenishment target stands higher at RM3 billion with year-to-date FY27 job wins standing at RM819.5 million.

items.title: Missing man last seen in Kota Damansara, police seek public help
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police are seeking public assistance to locate a man reported missing in Kota Damansara. Petaling Jaya district deputy police chief Superintendent M Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said a report was lodged at 9.52am on Wednesday regarding the disappearance of Cheah Yew Wei, 33. Those with information are urged to contact investigating officer Sergeant Major Roszaidi Abdul Talib at 019-3778383 or the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters operations room at 03-79662222.

items.title: After call with Pezeshkian, Anwar denounces ‘US-backed Israeli attacks’ on Iran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has denounced in the strongest terms the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran, following a phone call with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to discuss the escalating conflict in the Middle East. In a statement released today, Anwar said he conveyed Malaysia’s deepest condolences to the people of Iran for the significant losses suffered, including high-level political and military figures as well as innocent civilians, children, and other non-combatants. “Malaysia condemns any action targeting civilians, as it clearly violates international law, humanitarian principles and the values of justice,” Anwar said.

items.title: Have you seen him? PJ police seek public’s help to trace missing man last spotted in Kota Damansara on March 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Police are appealing for the public’s help in locating a local man who was reported missing after he was last seen at his residence in D’Rimba apartment, Kota Damansara, Petaling Jaya on March 15. Petaling Jaya deputy district police chief Supt. “The Petaling Jaya district police headquarters (IPD) received a report of the man’s disappearance at 9.52am, Wednesday.

items.title: Malaysia reaffirms support for regional efforts to ease tensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysia hopes that ceasefire efforts between Afghanistan and Pakistan will lead to lasting stability grounded in compassion and solidarity, said Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. He said this was conveyed during a telephone conversation with Afghanistan’s Acting Prime Minister Mullah Mohammad Hasan Akhund, where they discussed developments in South Asia, particularly tensions between the two countries and ongoing ceasefire efforts. He added that Malaysia remains consistent in supporting diplomatic efforts, including those undertaken by countries such as Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye.

items.title: Malaysia explores nuclear power as Middle East war drives up energy costs
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Amar Fadillah Yusof said the move, which is part of the 13th Malaysia Plan, is a crucial step towards strengthening the nation’s long-term energy security and supporting its clean energy goals. “The need to assess the potential and feasibility of nuclear energy is increasingly relevant in the context of a changing global energy landscape, influenced by geopolitical uncertainty as well as instability in fuel supply and prices,” Fadillah, who is also the minister of energy transition and water transformation, said in a statement today. Today, Fadillah said the government sees nuclear energy as a stable, low-carbon power source that can reduce Malaysia’s heavy dependence on fossil fuels, which have become increasingly volatile.

items.title: Police: Kuala Terengganu pensioner duped of RM96,000 in long-running online investment scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, March 27 — A 63-year-old female pensioner lost RM96,000 after falling victim to an online investment scam that promised lucrative returns. Kuala Terengganu police chief Azli Mohd Noor said the victim first came across a Labuan-based investment advertisement on WhatsApp in 2022. He said the victim, who is from Kuala Terengganu, contacted the suspect and invested a total of RM96,000 through 50 transactions to 15 different bank accounts between 2022 and March 14 this year, using her savings and pension funds.

items.title: Food delivery rider dies after crashing into stalled car on LDP
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 27 — A 38-year-old food delivery rider was killed after his motorcycle crashed into a stationary car at Kilometre 3 of the Damansara-Puchong Highway (LDP) here yesterday. Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Supt M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said police were notified of the incident at approximately 1.50 pm. Anyone with information can contact traffic investigating officer Inspector Jayseelan Suppaya at 011-1025 2531 or the Petaling Jaya District Police Headquarters at 03-7966 2222.

items.title: Just five offences found in 413 bus checks during Aidilfitri operations in Terengganu, says state JPJ
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, March 27 — Only five out of 413 express buses inspected were penalised during the first 11 days of Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026 (Ops HRA 2026) in Terengganu. Terengganu Road Transport Department (JPJ) director Mohd Zamri Samion said the offences involved continuous use of the right lane, cracked windscreens, worn tyres and other technical issues. “We found that compliance among operators and express bus drivers with safety regulations is very high, with no serious cases reported,” he said during an Ops HRA 2026 programme at the Kuala Terengganu City Council (MBKT) bus terminal last night.

items.title: Anwar backs Egypt’s push for de-escalation as Middle East tensions intensify
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysia appreciates Egypt’s contribution in urging the conflicting parties towards de-escalation, dialogue and an immediate end to violence in West Asia, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, in a Facebook post last night, said he conveyed the message during his telephone conversation with Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi yesterday, which also saw him expressing Malaysia’s commitment to strengthening bilateral relations between the two countries to the level of a Strategic Partnership. The prime minister said he also expressed Malaysia’s concern over the current conflict in West Asia and took the opportunity to convey Eid greetings to El-Sisi.

items.title: KL JPJ inspects over 9,500 vehicles, seizes 66 motorcycles in Aidilfitri crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — A total of 9,542 vehicles were inspected and 66 motorcycles seized by the Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department (KL JPJ) during four special motorcycle operations in conjunction with Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026. In a statement yesterday, KL JPJ said that the Ops Khas Motosikal operations were conducted in line with the festive season enforcement directive, focusing on road safety and smooth traffic flow under the slogan Pandu Cermat, Sampai Selamat (Drive Carefully, Arrive Safely). It said the main offences detected included driving without a valid licence (CDL), expired Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM), lack of insurance coverage, failure to use designated motorcycle lanes, improper registration numbers and illegal vehicle modifications.

items.title: Low dam levels spark alarm across Malaysia ahead of predicted record-breaking El Niño
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — With a potentially record-breaking El Niño phenomenon looming, nearly a quarter of Malaysia’s dams, particularly in the northern and southern states, are already reporting cautionary low water levels, prompting urgent calls for nationwide water conservation efforts. Experts are warning that the current stress on water resources, driven by a prolonged hot and dry spell, could escalate into a significant water security challenge when the El Niño event is expected to hit in the coming months, The Straits Times reported. Charles Santiago, a water governance advocate and former member of parliament, said the government must act immediately to communicate the risk to the public.

items.title: Timah Tasoh Dam not yet critical, but dry conditions raise concern in Perlis
items.summary: Perlis Department of Irrigation and Drainage (JPS) director Amir Budiman Abdullah said the dam’s water level reading this morning stood at 26.72 metres, which has not reached the critical level. “Our advice to water users in Perlis, regardless of sector, is to practice prudent water usage and avoid wastage, using water only when necessary,” he said briefly to Bernama via WhatsApp yesterday. Earlier, the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, performed the Istisqa’ prayer (prayer for rain) with nearly 1,000 congregants, including state leaders, at the Istiadat Square, Istana Arau, in Arau yesterday morning.

items.title: Shah Alam police hunt two suspects over Proton Wira theft from Section 33 premises
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 27 — Police are actively tracking down two men believed to be involved in the theft of a Proton Wira at a premises in Section 33 here on Tuesday. Shah Alam District Police chief ACP Ramsay Embol said a report on the incident was received at 3.12 am from the vehicle owner, a local man in his 20s. “CCTV footage showed two male suspects believed to have used another vehicle to steal the car.

items.title: Malaysia’s pest control prices set to rise as diesel hike threatens essential fogging services, industry warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — The Malaysian Pest Management Association (MPMA) has issued an urgent warning that the surge in diesel prices will likely force pest control operators to increase their charges, a move that could impact public health and food safety nationwide. “The sharp rise in diesel costs has therefore placed significant and compounding pressure on operating margins for all sizes of Pest Control Service providers in Malaysia,” the association said. Malaysia Pest Management Association president Regine Lim speaks during an interview in Petaling Jaya on June 14, 2024.

items.title: Malaysia’s hotels pivot to local and Asian markets as Iran war triggers cancellations
items.summary: To counter any loss in hotel bookings from overseas tourists, Toh said MAH is working together with Tourism Malaysia to encourage travel among those living in Malaysia in line with Visit Malaysia Year 2026. MAH has over 1,000 members, while Tourism Malaysia’s latest online statistics showed there are 5,334 hotels in Malaysia with nearly 350,000 hotel rooms as of September 2025. Based on Tourism Malaysia’s annual figures from 2016 to 2024, the bulk of hotel guests are usually those travelling within Malaysia, while international travellers typically account for around a third of hotel guests.

items.title: JPJ: Driving licences expired more than 36 months automatically disqualify holders from BUDI95
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 26 — Malaysian Driving Licence (LMM) holders whose licences have expired for more than 36 months will not qualify for the targeted BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) subsidy, said Road Transport Department (RTD) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Within this period, holders remain eligible for the BUDI95 subsidy,” he said. Aedy Fadly also emphasised that anyone driving a motor vehicle must ensure their driving licence remains valid and has not expired.
